items.title: Speed up scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under Poisons Act
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Authorities and regulators must fast-track the scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under the Poisons Act 1952 to ensure unimpeded legal action against offenders. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said swift action was critical, warning that delays in gazetting these substances would severely hamper police investigations. "When a police investigation traces a case back to synthetic drugs, the matter is referred to the Chemistry Department.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s strong ties and political stability helping secure energy supplies
items.summary: SIPITANG, June 13 — Malaysia’s strong international relations and political stability have enabled the country to secure continued access to oil and gas supplies despite growing uncertainty arising from the global energy crisis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said Malaysia’s prudent diplomatic approach had allowed countries such as Russia and Turkmenistan to regard Malaysia as a close and trusted partner. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.

items.title: Low UNHCR resettlement rate contributing to prolonged Rohingya presence in Malaysia, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — The low rate of third-country resettlement by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) is among the factors contributing to the prolonged presence of refugees, including the Rohingya, within local communities. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said that while UNHCR has two core responsibilities - registering refugees and securing third-country resettlement — statistics show that the number of refugee cards issued by the agency far exceeds the number of individuals successfully resettled abroad. As a result, while waiting to be resettled, these refugees remain within local communities,” he said.

items.title: New projects should include long-term maintenance plans, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh said governments needed to devote more attention and resources to maintaining existing public facilities, adding that the long-term costs of upkeep should be factored into decisions on new developments. Speaking at the launch of Kuala Lumpur Sustainability Week (KLSW) today, she said the challenge was not merely constructing new amenities, but ensuring they could continue serving the public years after completion. According to The Star , Yeoh said proposals for new developments should be accompanied by clear plans for their long-term maintenance.

items.title: Reset convention draws 20,000-strong crowd
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: The inaugural 'Reset' Convention drew an overwhelming response today, with attendance reaching nearly 20,000 people — double the organisers' initial target. Tanah Merah member of parliament Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz described the turnout as a strong show of support for the movement's aspirations and reform agenda. Also in attendance were Kelantan Pas commissioner Datuk Ahmad Yakub, former opposition leader and Reset movement founder Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, former Bersatu Melaka chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahim Thamby Chik and former Bersatu information chief Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan. Other attendees included Masjid Tanah MP Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin, Beluran MP Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee and Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal.

items.title: Anwar orders swift rollout of RM250,000 aid for Sipitang schools, rejects project delays
items.summary: SIPITANG, June 13 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today approved an immediate special allocation of RM250,000 for development purposes, including repair works on basic facilities at secondary schools in the Sipitang district. He said details on the implementation of the assistance and the disbursement of the funds would be coordinated immediately by the Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister’s Department. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.

items.title: Anwar approves immediate RM250,000 infrastructure allocation for Sipitang schools
items.summary: SIPITANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today approved an immediate special allocation of RM250,000 for infrastructure development, including repairs on basic facilities at secondary schools in the Sipitang district. He said the implementation of the assistance and the disbursement of funds would be coordinated immediately by the Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister's Department. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.

items.title: Don't rely on opposition's weaknesses to win state polls, Fahmi tells PH
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Pakatan Harapan (PH) must rely on its own strengths rather than banking on the opposition's weaknesses to secure victories in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections. PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the opposition's vulnerabilities should not compromise the coalition's election machinery preparations or lead to complacency. "Voters rely on both mass media and social media.

items.title: Zahid defends Umno’s solo bid in Negeri Sembilan, rejects betrayal claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Umno has rejected allegations of betrayal levelled by Negeri Sembilan caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, with party president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi saying the party’s support for Aminuddin’s administration was only intended to last until the dissolution of the state assembly. He said that after Aminuddin announced the dissolution of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly on June 5, Umno decided to contest the upcoming state election independently. His remarks came a day after Aminuddin accused Umno of betraying its allies and urged voters to punish the party in the Negeri Sembilan state election on August 1.

items.title: Hazmat team deployed after chemical spill in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN: A hazardous materials (Hazmat) team was deployed after a tanker carrying hydrochloric acid overturned near the Cherating bridge here today, causing part of its load to spill into a nearby mangrove swamp. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the Gebeng fire station received an alert at 7.32am and dispatched nine firefighters to the scene. Salahuddin said firefighters inspecting the site found a leak from the storage tank's manhole cover, with hydrochloric acid flowing into a nearby mangrove swamp.

items.title: Zahid calls on Muslim scholars to turn ideas into policies and solutions for the ummah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on Muslim scholars to spearhead an ‘Intellectual Diplomacy’ agenda that actively translates consensus into concrete policy and real-world solutions for the ummah. A model that embraces progress while preserving human values,” he said at the opening of the 4th Southeast Asian Scholars Conference 2026, co-organised by the Muslim World League and the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) here today. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the Minister of Rural and Regional Development, said Southeast Asia, with its moderate Islamic tradition and diverse societies, is uniquely positioned to offer an Ethical Technology Model that embraces progress without eroding identity.

items.title: Malaysian Judiciary warns of fake debt notice threatening bank account freeze
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — The Malaysian Judiciary has warned the public against a fake document circulating online that falsely claims to have been issued by the Office of the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court of Malaysia. The judiciary stressed that the document was fraudulent and had not been issued by the Office of the Chief Registrar. “The Office of the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court of Malaysia confirms that the document is fake and was not issued by this office,” it said.

items.title: Tiong: Expand community engagement programmes to strengthen public understanding of military’s role
items.summary: SIBU, June 13 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing has called on the Malaysian Army to expand community engagement programmes to strengthen public understanding of the military’s role and foster patriotism. He said initiatives such as the Jelajah Wira Madani programme should be expanded as they help the public better appreciate the sacrifices and contributions of military personnel in safeguarding the nation’s sovereignty. “I hope more programmes of this nature will be organised in the future, including those involving male and female military veterans,” he said at the Gawai Day celebration held in conjunction with the Jelajah Wira Madani 2026 programme at Dewan RASCOM, RASCOM Camp 9th Brigade, here today.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution says Poisons Act updates must keep pace with emerging synthetic drugs
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — Authorities and regulators face a growing challenge to fast-track the scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under the Poisons Act 1952, a move critical to ensuring unimpeded legal action. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said swift action was critical, warning that delays in gazetting the substances would severely hamper police investigations. “When a police investigation traces a case back to synthetic drugs, the matter is referred to the Chemistry Department.

items.title: Asyraf Wajdi says poor students will get help, urges them to accept offers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has urged students from poor and underprivileged backgrounds not to reject university offers due to financial concerns, assuring them that assistance is available. "I have contacted several vice-chancellors, and Minister Dr Zambry has instructed universities to help students facing financial difficulties. Asyraf also assured students who received offers from Mara institutions, including Kolej Mara, Kolej Profesional Mara, TVET Mara, Kolej Poly-Tech Mara, Universiti Poly-Tech Malaysia and Universiti Kuala Lumpur, that support would be provided to those in need.

items.title: Johor voters should judge candidates by service and commitment, says Azalina
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 13 — Umno information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said has urged voters to choose candidates who are close to the people, capable of providing the best service and who place the interests of their constituencies as a priority in the Johor state election on July 11. Azalina, who is also the Pengerang MP, expressed hope that incumbent Barisan Nasional state assemblymen who had performed well during the past term would be retained as candidates for the Johor polls. The Election Commission has set July 11 as polling day for the 16th Johor state election, with nomination day on June 27 and early voting on July 7.

items.title: [UPDATED] Sabah southern link project reflects federal commitment to Sabah's interior, says PM
items.summary: SIPITANG: The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) electricity grid project reflects the federal government's commitment to ensuring infrastructure development reaches communities in Sabah's interior. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the project was in line with the Malaysia Madani policy, which emphasises balanced and equitable development rather than concentrating progress solely in major urban centres. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.

items.title: AirBorneo says operations improving as affected aircraft return to service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — AirBorneo is beginning to see gradual improvements in its operations as affected aircraft return to service, while efforts continue to restore operational stability following a series of flight disruptions across Sarawak and Sabah. Its chief executive officer Megat Ardian Wira Mohd Aminuddin, said the airline is making steady progress and remains focused on improving reliability as quickly as possible. While airline operations involve maintenance providers, parts suppliers and aircraft manufacturers, Megat Ardian Wira noted that passengers ultimately identify the airline as the service provider.

items.title: Hundreds gather at Thai royal palace to bid farewell to Princess Bajrakitiyabha
items.summary: BANGKOK: Hundreds of black-clad Thai mourners gathered at the royal palace in Bangkok on Saturday, awaiting the arrival of the body of the king's eldest daughter a day after her death was announced. Princess Bajrakitiyabha, known as Princess Bha, died aged 47 on Thursday after spending more than three years in a coma due to a cardiac condition. "I will wait here all day for her body to arrive," Donnapha Kladbupha, a 54-year-old English teacher and self-described royalist, told AFP.

items.title: Azalina to seek 2027 Budget allocation to expand rural legal aid satellite offices
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said will seek an allocation under the 2027 Budget to expand Legal Aid Department (JBG) satellite offices to rural areas nationwide. She said the move is important to ensure that the public, particularly those in remote and rural areas, have easier access to legal aid services and are aware of their rights to legal protection and assistance. "When we talk about legal aid, we must provide protection to the people so that they know they have the right to obtain legal assistance.

items.title: Kelantan records highest drug abuse rate in 2025 as border states remain most affected, says AADK
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 13 — Four states located near or along the country’s borders continued to record the highest rates of drug abuse in Malaysia in 2025. The National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) Prevention Division director Siti Mariam Mursidan said Kelantan recorded the highest rate, with 1,105 drug addicts and abusers per 100,000 population, followed by Terengganu (997), Perlis (970) and Kedah (965). “Apart from these four states, other states that also recorded relatively high rates were Pahang with 795 cases per 100,000 population, Penang (757), Melaka (659) and Perak (579).

items.title: Malaysia ready to deploy peacekeepers if UN extends Unifil mandate, says Khaled
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Malaysia may continue deploying a peacekeeping force to Lebanon under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mission if required. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the matter is being discussed with the United Nations (UN) following Lebanon's deteriorating security situation due to the ongoing conflict involving Israel. "Malaysia had previously planned to end its peacekeeping involvement as soon as the Unifil mandate expires this December.

items.title: 'Respect your friends', Zahid tells DAP Youth chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has reminded coalition partners to be respectful in public discourse after a DAP leader labelled the party hypocritical. The remark came after DAP Youth chief Woo Kah Leong described Umno as hypocritical and arrogant over Johor Umno's plan to contest the upcoming Johor election on its own rather than cooperate with DAP. Zahid said political partners should avoid making reckless statements that could strain relationships within the coalition.

items.title: Penang clinic joins first phase of national lung health initiative using AI screening
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, June 13 — The Seberang Jaya Health Clinic here has been selected as a facility for the first phase of the National Lung Health Initiative in Penang, aimed at strengthening the prevention, early screening and treatment of various lung diseases. Penang Health director Datuk Dr Fazilah Shaik Allaudin said the new Ministry of Health (MOH) initiative involves the use of artificial intelligence (AI) technology to assist in chest X-ray screening more quickly and accurately, particularly for the early detection of lung cancer. Dr Fazilah said the MOH is also continuing to strengthen various initiatives to help the public, particularly teenagers, quit smoking and overcome nicotine addiction through the JomQuit Programme, Smoking Cessation Clinics, the Oral Health Without Smoking Practice Programme (KOTAK), and the AI-based digital platform, Cik Era.

items.title: Reset movement to announce new party, Dewan Rakyat plan today
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: The Reset movement is expected to announce several major developments, including the formation of a new political party and a parliamentary arrangement ahead of the next Dewan Rakyat sitting. Tasik Gelugor member of parliament and former Bersatu leader Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan said the announcements would be made by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang and Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. "God willing, we will announce details on a new party formed through the Reset movement, as well as the arrangement in the Dewan Rakyat for the upcoming parliamentary sitting," he told the New Straits Times when met at the Reset Convention here today.

items.title: Azalina to seek Budget 2027 allocation to expand legal aid to rural communities
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 13 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said will seek an allocation under Budget 2027 to expand the establishment of Legal Aid Department (JBG) satellite offices to rural areas nationwide. She said the move is important to ensure that the public, particularly those in remote and rural areas, have easier access to legal aid services and are aware of their rights to legal protection and assistance. “When we talk about legal aid, we must provide protection to the people so that they know they have the right to obtain legal assistance.

items.title: Fadhlina: Govt committed to supporting all education streams fairly
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The government remains committed to supporting all education pathways fairly and inclusively, regardless of institution or background, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said today. "All groups must be given due consideration, not only UEC students, but also others, including those from institutions outside the national education stream. "We want to support all education streams so that our education system is elevated on an equal basis, ensuring fairness for all and recognising that education is for Malaysian children," she said.

items.title: 'Set the date': Albert Tei accepts J-KOM chief's debate challenge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Businessman and social media personality Albert Tei today accepted a debate challenge from Community Communications Department (J-Kom) director-general Mohd Hisyamuddin Ghazali. "I am ready," he said, urging Hisyamuddin to set a date, venue and moderator. Speaking at a protest outside TikTok Malaysia's office today, Tei said he was ready to face the debate following remarks allegedly made by Hisyamuddin when they met outside the Putrajaya district police headquarters earlier this week.

items.title: Anwar says Sabah power grid project will bring development to interior regions
items.summary: SIPITANG, June 13 — The implementation of the Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) electricity grid project reflects the federal government’s commitment to ensuring that development and basic infrastructure reach communities in Sabah’s interior. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the project was in line with the Malaysia Madani policy, which emphasises balanced and equitable development rather than concentrating progress solely in major urban centres. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.

items.title: No utility services for illegal structures, says Nga
items.summary: Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said this is to prevent the emergence of illegal settlements, including those linked to foreign communities such as the Rohingya. "However, the government's policy is clear. Meanwhile, he said the government has revoked 26,108 business licences operated by foreign nationals through 4,000 enforcement operations carried out nationwide as of May this year.

items.title: Over 54,000 Johor oil palm smallholders secure MSPO certification
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: More than 54,000 Johor oil palm smallholders have obtained the Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil (MSPO) certification, strengthening the country's position in the global market. "It also serves as an important shield against unfounded allegations and negative perceptions directed at the country's palm oil industry," she said after officiating the Batu Pahat Oil Palm Smallholders MSPO Strengthening programme at Dewan Peladang Sri Medan here today. Noraini said smallholders remain the backbone of the national palm oil industry, contributing significantly to local economies and rural livelihoods.

items.title: Police investigate after three-year-old found alone by roadside at 2am in Kota Samarahan
items.summary: KUCHING, June 13 — A toddler aged around three-years-old was found alone and crying for help by the roadside in Taman Berlian, Kota Samarahan. The child has since been taken to Samarahan police station before being sent to the Sarawak General Hospital (SGH) emergency unit for a medical check-up. Samarahan district police acting chief DSP Barry William has confirmed the incident, saying that an investigation has been launched to determine how the girl ended up by the main road.

items.title: No question of betrayal after term ends, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi rejected claims that the party betrayed its allies by choosing to contest separately in the Negri Sembilan election, saying there was no question of disloyalty once a government's term had ended. He said Barisan Nasional's support for the Negri Sembilan government remained in place until the end of its term and that the coalition would continue working within the political alliance while seeking to strengthen its position in the state assembly. "There is no betrayal because our support for the government in Negri Sembilan is until the end of the term.

items.title: Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta arrives in Malaysia for five-day special visit
items.summary: SEPANG: Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta arrived in Malaysia today for a five-day special visit. Also present to welcome the president were Wisma Putra Chief of Protocol Ikram Mohd Ibrahim and Ceremonial and International Conference Secretariat Division Chief of Ceremony Datuk Rozainor Ramli. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will host a luncheon in his honour at the Seri Perdana Complex on Tuesday.

items.title: Protesters warn of larger rallies if alleged TikTok restrictions continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Protesters outside TikTok Malaysia's office have warned of larger demonstrations if the platform continues what they claim are restrictions on content critical of the government. The gathering, dubbed "Himpunan Gegar TikTok", involved social media personalities, content creators, and politicians, who accused the platform of unfairly restricting accounts and content expressing dissenting views. If they continue blocking accounts, there will be Himpunan Gegar TikTok 2, Himpunan Gegar TikTok 3 and more gatherings in the future," he said.

items.title: Defence Ministry orders tighter security for Malbatt after Lebanon airstrike incident
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Defence Ministry has instructed Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 personnel to tighten security measures following an airstrike that left two peacekeepers with minor injuries in Lebanon on Thursday. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the directive was crucial to ensure the safety of Malaysian peacekeepers serving in the conflict zone. He clarified that the injuries were caused by bomb splinters landing near the personnel's location rather than a direct strike near Haris in southern Lebanon.

items.title: 'Greatest courage': Fadhlina praises PM's education policies
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek today praised the Madani administration's commitment to inclusive education policies, saying they ensure no group is left behind. Fadhlina said the government remained steadfast in strengthening an education system that guarantees equal opportunities for students regardless of race, religion or background. Fadhlina said the government's commitment to education extended to all schools, including Chinese vernacular schools, through infrastructure development and continued support.

items.title: Visually impaired fisherman beaten to death with log
items.summary: TAWAU: A visually impaired fisherman was beaten to death with a mangrove log during an argument while repairing a house in Felda Umas near here on Thursday morning. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Jasmin Hussin said initial investigations showed that the victim, a registered person with disabilities (PwD), was involved in an argument with the two suspects while working on a resident's house. Jasmin added that a police team from the Tawau district Criminal Investigation Department tracked down and arrested two foreign men, aged 23 and 52, on Bingkulan Island in Kalabakan to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Anwar: No reason to bring down Aminuddin-led Negri govt
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has questioned the motives behind attempts to topple the Negri Sembilan government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. he asked when launching the Negri Sembilan PH election machinery here yesterday. Anwar, who is also the prime minister, said he has repeatedly reminded all quarters to maintain political stability and allow the government to fulfil its duties to the people.

items.title: Fadhlina Sidek hands over approval letter for new SJKC Heng Ee building to provide more conducive learning environment
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 13 — Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek today handed over the letter of approval for the construction of a new building for Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina (SJKC) Heng Ee, which will be developed within the jurisdiction of the Barat Daya district education office (PPD). She said the construction of the new building is important to provide more comfortable and conducive educational infrastructure, in line with growing demand from residents and the local community. “Efforts to improve conducive school infrastructure will support the education agenda, particularly the implementation of the new curriculum next year and the Malaysia Education Blueprint.

items.title: Onn Hafiz's rejection of DAP is not about race, says Khaled
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's rejection of DAP has nothing to do with race, says Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. Khaled, the Umno vice-president, said Barisan Nasional has always positioned itself as a multiracial coalition that includes parties representing the Chinese community. "He did not say that DAP is Chinese and Chinese is DAP.

items.title: Govt to block water and electricity for illegal structures, including Rohingya settlements, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: IPOH, June 13 — Buildings constructed without planning permission are illegal and will not be allowed access to water or electricity supplies, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said. He said the policy was intended to prevent the emergence of illegal settlements, including those linked to foreign communities such as the Rohingya. Meanwhile, he said the government had revoked 26,108 business licences operated by foreign nationals through 4,000 enforcement operations carried out nationwide as of May this year.

items.title: Defence minister confirms two Malbatt peacekeepers injured in Lebanon, says condition stable
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 13 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has instructed personnel of the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 to strengthen security measures and remain vigilant after two Malaysian peacekeepers in Lebanon sustained minor injuries from bomb splinters following an airstrike on Thursday. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the move was important to ensure the safety of Malaysian peacekeepers currently serving in the conflict area. Mohamed Khaled also said the United Nations Security Council is reassessing the position of peacekeeping forces in Lebanon, as the mandate of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon is scheduled to expire in December.

items.title: Fadhlina coy on extra seats for women in Johor, Negri polls
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: PKR Wanita chief Fadhlina Sidek remained tight-lipped on whether the party would allocate additional seats for women candidates in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections. The Election Commission (EC) has set July 11 as polling day for the Johor election , with nomination and early voting fixed for June 27 and July 7 respectively. For the Negri Sembilan election , polling will be held on Aug 1, while nomination and early voting are scheduled for July 18 and July 28 respectively.

items.title: Khaled: BN readiness more important than timing of Johor, Negri polls
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional's election preparedness will determine its victory in Johor and Negri Sembilan rather than the timing of the two polls, says Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. The Umno vice-president said when the Johor assembly was dissolved on June 1, the matter of concurrent elections did not arise as the Negri Sembilan assembly was still active. The Kota Tinggi member of parliament was asked if holding the Johor election separately from Negri Sembilan would give BN an advantage.

items.title: Timor-Leste President Ramos-Horta arrives in Malaysia for five-day visit
items.summary: SEPANG, June 13 — Timor- Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta arrived in Malaysia today for a five-day special visit. Also present to welcome the President were Wisma Putra Chief of Protocol Ikram Mohd Ibrahim and Chief of Ceremony of the Ceremonial and International Conference Secretariat Division Datuk Rozainor Ramli. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will host a luncheon in his honour at the Seri Perdana Complex on Tuesday.

items.title: Factory worker killed in three-vehicle crash after motorcycle skids into oncoming traffic
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A factory worker was killed after being struck by two cars in a three-vehicle collision along Jalan Pintasan Semeling here last night. The victim in the 9pm crash was identified as motorcyclist Muhammad Hafizi Muhammad Rizal, 22, from Penaga, Seberang Perai Utara, Penang. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said initial investigations revealed that two cars, a Perodua Axia and a Perodua Bezza, driven by two women, were travelling from Lembah Bujang towards Semeling.

items.title: Education minister: Schools told to act fast on sexual misconduct complaints
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 13 — School administrators, district education offices (PPD), and state education departments (JPN) have been urged to take firm and immediate action as soon as they receive complaints of misconduct, particularly those involving sexual harassment of students. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said such cases must not be taken lightly or allowed to persist until they go viral before action is taken. “When a child reports being sexually harassed, parents and teachers must take action.

items.title: 18 Bersatu MPs subjected to disciplinary action rally behind Hamzah at convention
items.summary: Among those expected are Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal, Ketereh MP Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor, Padang Rengas MP Capt (Rtd) Datuk Azahari Hasan, Gerik MP Fathul Huzir Ayob and former Tasek Gelugor MP Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan, all of whom had faced disciplinary action by Bersatu. Sungai Besar MP Datuk Muslimin Yahaya, who was not among those expelled or suspended, is also expected to attend. "We came to show our support for Datuk Seri Hamzah.

items.title: Mindanao earthquake: 5.2-magnitude quake hits Philippines days after deadly disaster
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A moderate earthquake measuring 5.2 on the Richter scale struck Mindanao in the Philippines at 10.05am today. The latest tremor comes days after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck southern Philippines, affecting hundreds of thousands of people in Mindanao, with tremors felt in parts of Tawau and Semporna in Sabah. As of Thursday, the Philippines' National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) reported 47 deaths, 31 people missing and more than 346,000 people affected by the disaster.

items.title: Melaka sees strong uptake as 1,318 women get free breast cancer screenings
items.summary: MELAKA, June 13 — A total of 1,318 women aged 40 to 70 across the state have undergone free mammogram screenings this year under the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN) Mammogram Subsidy programme. State Women, Family and Community Development Committee chairman Datuk Kalsom Noordin said that the breast cancer screening programme remains open to women within this age group, with a target of 1,800 women undergoing the test this year. “So far, we have seen a very encouraging response from women in Melaka towards undergoing mammogram screening through this initiative, compared to previous years.

items.title: MetMalaysia confirms no tsunami threat after 5.2 quake off Mindanao
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — A moderate earthquake measuring 5.2 on the Richter scale struck Mindanao, the Philippines, at 10.05 am today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the quake occurred at a depth of 10 kilometres (km), about 101 km south of Koronadal City. The latest quake comes days after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck southern Philippines, affecting hundreds of thousands of people in Mindanao, with tremors felt in parts of Tawau and Semporna in Sabah.

items.title: Khaled: Separate state polls offer no edge to BN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will have to depend on its own strength to win over voters, regardless of whether the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are held separately or simultaneously, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said today. Khaled said Umno and BN had made preparations to face any election. “When Johor dissolved its state assembly before this, there was no issue of holding the state election simultaneously with Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: Foreigner with 34 outstanding summonses arrested after fleeing RTD operation
items.summary: PENDANG: A foreigner with 34 outstanding traffic summonses was arrested after fleeing a Road Transport Department (RTD) enforcement operation in Guar Kepayang here yesterday afternoon. In the 6.30pm incident, the Rohingya abandoned his motorcycle and fled through a padi field upon spotting the enforcement officers. Kedah RTD director Stien Van Lutam said the motorcycle was seized.

items.title: Sarawak internet coverage rises to 94.5 pc after RM2.3b investment under Saluran
items.summary: KUCHING, June 13 — Internet coverage across Sarawak has increased significantly to 94.5 per cent from 54.3 per cent in 2022 following a RM2.3 billion investment in telecommunications infrastructure under the Sarawak Linking Urban, Rural and Nation (Saluran) initiative. Julaihi, who is also Sacofa Sdn Bhd chairman, said digital connectivity had been identified as a key enabler under the Post COVID-19 Development Strategy (PCDS) 2030 and the Sarawak Digital Economy Blueprint 2030. He highlighted the Sarawak Multimedia Authority Rural Transformation (Smart Project as one of the key components of the Saluran initiative, involving the construction of 618 telecommunications towers, including 17 relay towers, in rural and remote areas throughout Sarawak.

items.title: PAS to discuss Johor, Negeri Sembilan strategy and possible alliances next week, says Samsuri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — PAS leaders will meet next week to determine the party’s approach to the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections, including whether to explore new political alignments, as Perikatan Nasional (PN) weighs its options ahead of the back-to-back contests. Harian Metro reported that PN chairman and Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said no decisions had been made on forming any new alliances, stressing that discussions within PAS’ top leadership would take place before the matter is brought to the coalition level. “PAS’ top leadership will hold discussions next week to determine how we will move forward.

items.title: Sanusi sets sights on 36-0 Kedah state election victory for PAS
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said PAS is targeting all 36 Kedah state seats in the next state election, while also seeking to capture the state’s only parliamentary constituency not currently under the party’s control. According to Harian Metro , the Kedah menteri besar said PAS would attempt to wrest the Sungai Petani parliamentary seat from Pakatan Harahan (PH), which would give the party all 15 parliamentary seats in the state after winning 14 in GE15. Muhammad Sanusi said the target was not impossible, citing PAS’ performance in Terengganu, where the party won all 32 state seats in the 2023 state election.

items.title: Sanusi says Anwar open to holding GE16 alongside polls in four Perikatan-led states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was receptive to the idea of holding the next general election simultaneously with polls in the four Perikatan Nasional-led states, arguing that the move would cut costs and reduce duplication of work. New Straits Times reported the PAS election director as saying he had raised the matter with Anwar, expressing hope that Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu and Perlis would go to the polls alongside the 16th general election (GE16). Holding them concurrently will save costs and ease the work of the Election Commission,” he told reporters after launching Kedah PAS’ election machinery in Kota Sarang Semut, Yan, last night.

items.title: Terengganu to act based on facts, not perception over Rohingya presence, says exco
items.summary: State Local Government, Housing and Health Committee chairman Datuk Wan Sukairi Wan Abdullah said such an approach is important to preserve community safety, public order and social harmony. However, he said the state government understands public concerns over the presence of the Rohingya community in several areas of the state. The state government will not act hastily or based solely on perceptions,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Eight houses, motorcycle destroyed in early morning fire in Kuala Perlis
items.summary: KANGAR, June 13 — Eight houses and a motorcycle were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Seberang Alor, Kuala Perlis, near here early this morning. Perlis Fire and Rescue Department director senior fire superintendent I Wan Mohd Hamizi Wan Mohd Zin said firefighters received an emergency call at 3.38 am, with a team from the Kuala Perlis Fire and Rescue Station arriving at the scene two minutes later. Fire fighters from the Kangar Fire and Rescue Station, together with volunteer fire brigades from Simpang Empat, Padang Besar, Kuala Perlis and Pauh, assisted in bringing the blaze under control.
